primary_diagnosis.name: Tinea cruris (dermatophytosis of the groin), steroid-modified (tinea incognito)

pathophysiology_summary: Tinea cruris is a dermatophyte infection of the groin, favoured by heat, sweat, friction and occlusion (heavy labourer in tight synthetic underwear), producing well-demarcated erythematous scaling plaques with a raised, arcuate, scaly advancing border and central (often reddish-brown) clearing, spreading onto the inner thighs and characteristically SPARING the scrotum and penis. A potent topical corticosteroid (betamethasone) suppressed local immunity and caused an explosive steroid-modified flare (tinea incognito) with some atrophy/maceration. Discriminators: scrotal sparing, an active scaly advancing border and absence of satellite pustules distinguish it from candidal intertrigo (beefy-red, satellite pustules, involves the scrotum); the raised scaly pruritic border distinguishes it from erythrasma (flat, mildly itchy, fine wrinkled scale, coral-red on Wood's lamp); and asymmetrical advancing borders with central clearing distinguish it from smooth non-scaly inverse psoriasis. Management is stopping the steroid, a topical antifungal (terbinafine) and — given the severity/extent/steroid modification — a short oral antifungal (terbinafine), a barrier/drying regimen, loose cotton underwear, and treating concurrent tinea pedis to prevent re-inoculation. Safety-net for secondary bacterial infection and monitor for steroid-induced atrophy.

differential.evidence_basis: Scaly advancing-border groin plaques with scrotal sparing, spreading down the thighs.
Beefy-red with satellite pustules and scrotal involvement — absent here.
Flat, mildly itchy, fine wrinkled scale — not a raised scaly pruritic advancing border.
Smooth shiny non-scaly plaques, not asymmetrical advancing scaly borders with clearing.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Recognise tinea cruris (scrotal sparing, scaly advancing border), STOP the potent steroid, and treat with topical ± short oral antifungal; treat concurrent tinea pedis.

differential.evidence_basis: Scaly advancing border, scrotal sparing, steroid-modified; no satellite pustules or secondary infection.
No satellite pustules and scrotal sparing argue against candida.
Working diagnosis: stop steroid, topical + short oral antifungal, drying/barrier care, loose cotton underwear, treat tinea pedis; infection safety-net.

red_flags.description: Secondary bacterial infection (hot/hard spreading swelling, thick foul pus, fever)

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Not stopping the steroid driving tinea incognito/atrophy.
Missing scrotal sparing/advancing scaly border and mistreating.
Leaving athlete's foot untreated, causing groin re-inoculation.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Stop the steroid, prescribe topical + a short oral antifungal (given severity/extent/steroid modification), drying/barrier and clothing advice, and treat concurrent tinea pedis; review/urgent care if secondary infection.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Continuing steroids, or mistreating as candida/erythrasma.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Tinea cruris: topical antifungal; oral antifungal for extensive/refractory/steroid-modified disease; stop topical corticosteroids; keep the area dry; treat concurrent tinea pedis.
